perturbation.original_text: 1. The term “Confidential information” for the purpose of this Agreement shall mean the said Submissions and each concept, idea, game-play mechanic, set design, business model, and/or other element contained therein and any and all other proprietary and/or any other information and/or data which is provided and/or obtained hereunder weather in relation to the submission and/or otherwise, whether in writing, pictorially, in machine readable form, orally or by observation during their interactions/discussions, in connection with the Purpose or otherwise, including but not limited to, all intangible and tangible information, documents, data, papers, statements, any business/customer information and trade secrets relating to its business practices in connection with the Purpose or otherwise, and will form a part of the proprietary and confidential information weather disclosed by the Disclosing and/or Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. The term “Confidential information” for the purpose of this Agreement shall mean information exchanged between parties. The specifics and scope of this information will be further defined in subsequent project-specific agreements. and will form a part of the proprietary and confidential information weather disclosed by the Disclosing and/or Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly reduces the scope of "Confidential Information" in Section 1 by removing detailed definitions of the submission types, ideas, and data initially included. This now only includes information exchanged between parties, that are intended to be defined in subsequent agreements, but no further agreements are explicitly mentioned in the NDA. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because later sections of the contract, such as those concerning permitted use and return of Confidential Information, still operate under the assumption that a specific and detailed definition of Confidential Information exists, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Receiving shall use the Confidential information only for the Purpose and not disclose any of the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, and in addition to the same the Receiving Party will only share such Confidential Information with its internal employees only and strictly on a need to know basis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of "Confidential Information" by removing specific examples and deferring to future agreements. The contradicted text (Section 3) assumes a detailed and well-defined understanding of "Confidential Information" when specifying permitted uses and restrictions on disclosure. Because the change makes the definition of Confidential Information in Section 1 less specific, Section 3 now operates with an undefined key term, which directly breaks compatibility and creates a strong in-text contradiction and ambiguity regarding the scope of confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 4. The Receiving Party shall hold and keep in strictest confidence any and all Confidential Information and shall treat the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care and protection as it would treat its own Confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. The Receiving Party shall hold and keep in strictest confidence any Confidential Information. The level of care required will be subject to mutual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Here, the change removes the standard of care previously set by referring to 'the same degree of care and protection as it would treat its own Confidential information.' The added phrase 'subject to mutual agreement' introduces uncertainty and necessitates a separate agreement on the level of care, making the clause less enforceable and creating a contradiction with the overall intent of the NDA. This makes the clause unenforceable, as no specific measure or care is specified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The Receiving party acknowledges that damages are not a sufficient remedy for the Disclosing Party for any breach of any of the Receiving Party’s undertakings herein provided and the Receiving Party further acknowledges that the Disclosing party is entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief (as appropriate) as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or any undertakings hereunder by the Receiving Party, in addition to and without prejudice any other remedies available to the Disclosing Party in law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a standard of care for confidential information. The changed text removes that standard and makes the level of care 'subject to mutual agreement,' introducing uncertainty. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a breach of the Receiving Party's undertakings can be readily determined and remedied with specific performance or injunctive relief. Because the level of care is now undetermined unless by mutual agreement, determining a breach becomes unclear, thus making specific performance difficult to pursue. The change creates a strong in-text contradiction that would cause confusion or unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The Receiving Party shall not copy or reproduce in any way (including without limitation, store in any computer or electronic system) any Confidential information or any documents containing Confidential information without the Disclosing party’s written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The Receiving Party may copy or reproduce Confidential information with standard security protocols.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits copying Confidential Information without written consent. The modified version allows copying with 'standard security protocols,' but this term is undefined in the agreement. This introduces ambiguity and the possibility of different interpretations of what constitutes 'standard security protocols,' thus weakening the protection of Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction because it undermines the confidentiality expected in other sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Receiving Party shall not modify or erase the logos, trademarks etc. of Disclosing Party or any third party present on the Confidential Information. Neither party shall use or display the logos, trademarks etc., of the other party in any advertisement, press etc. and/or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited copying confidential information without written consent, while the changed text allows copying with 'standard security protocols'. This contradicts the contradicted text (Section 10) which requires written consent before using or displaying logos and trademarks found within the confidential information. The modified text allows copying which might involve logos and trademarks without written consent, which contradicts the explicit requirement for written permission stated in section 10.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The Receiving Party shall immediately upon request by the Disclosing party deliver back to the Disclosing Party all Confidential information disclosed to the Receiving party, including all copies(if any) mode under above clauses.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Upon termination of discussions, the Receiving Party will return Confidential information to the Disclosing Party, schedules will be mutually determined.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'schedules will be mutually determined' makes the return of Confidential Information subject to negotiation, which creates potential delays. By removing the immediacy of the return and adding the need for scheduling agreement, the change introduces uncertainty and potentially weakens the obligation to protect the confidentiality of the information. This weakens the obligation of returning the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. All obligations respecting the Confidential information already provided hereunder shall survive in perpetuity after the date that the specific Confidential information was first disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'schedules will be mutually determined' for returning confidential information, which directly contradicts the contradicted text stating that 'All obligations respecting the Confidential information... shall survive in perpetuity'. The original text obligated the Receiving Party to immediately return the information. The modified text weakens this obligation by making it subject to negotiation, potentially delaying or impeding the return, thereby creating a conflict with the perpetual survival of confidentiality obligations as stated in Clause 19. This is an example of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, since an essential language has been removed.

perturbation.original_text: 11. No warranties of any kind are given with respect to the Confidential information and/or any other information disclosed under this Agreement or any use thereof, except as may be otherwise agreed to in writing. Neither party shall be liable to the other hereunder for amounts representing loss of profits, loss of business or indirect, consequential or punitive damages of the other party in connection with the provision or use of Confidential information hereunder except to the extent that such provision or use is caused by and constitutes a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. No warranties are given with respect to disclosed information. Liability is limited to direct damages only.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except as may be otherwise agreed to in writing' and the clause disclaiming liability for loss of profits, loss of business, or indirect, consequential, or punitive damages, this change now creates a more limited liability framework. While seemingly simplifying the clause, it introduces ambiguity because, without the exceptions and specific exclusions, the scope of liability is unclear. This change contradicts the original intent of the agreement to limit liability, and now leaves the contract more open to interpretation and potential for disputes. This could mean it is easier to dispute since more damage types are included. It creates an in-text contradiction by removing the previous clarification on the types of damages that are not covered, potentially exposing the party to liabilities that were initially intended to be waived.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The Receiving party acknowledges that damages are not a sufficient remedy for the Disclosing Party for any breach of any of the Receiving Party’s undertakings herein provided and the Receiving Party further acknowledges that the Disclosing party is entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief (as appropriate) as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or any undertakings hereunder by the Receiving Party, in addition to and without prejudice any other remedies available to the Disclosing Party in law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11 significantly narrows the liability framework compared to the original text. The original text specifically excluded liability for loss of profits, loss of business, or indirect, consequential, or punitive damages, and included an 'except as may be otherwise agreed to in writing' clause, giving flexibility. The changed text removes these exclusions and limits liability to direct damages only. This contradicts Section 8, which acknowledges that damages are not a sufficient remedy and entitles the Disclosing party to specific performance or injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies. By limiting liability to direct damages, the changed text potentially removes the possibility of specific performance or injunctive relief as remedies for breach, creating a contradiction with Section 8's assertion that such remedies are available. This is a direct conflict, making the contract internally inconsistent and thus, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14. The Parties hereto undertake that any dispute which may arise between them shall first be dealt with in the manner stated below, irrespective of the other recourse, which any Party may have in law or in equity. 14.1 If any dispute arises between the Parties hereto during the subsistence of this Agreement or thereafter, in connection with the validity, interpretation, implementation or alleged material breach of any provision of this Agreement or regarding a question, including the questions as to whether the termination of this Agreement by one Party hereto has been legitimate, both Parties hereto shall endeavour to settle such dispute amicably. If the Parties fail to bring about an amicable settlement within a period of thirty (30) days, either Party to the dispute may give ten (10) days notice of invocation of dispute settlement by the Film Writers Association, Mumbai, to the other Party in writing.The Parties hereto shall submit to such mediation award by the Film Writers Association and the award shall be enforceable in any competent court of law in Mumbai.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. The Parties agree to resolve disputes through standard legal channels.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed dispute resolution process involving amicable settlement, mediation by the Film Writers Association, and specific notice periods, this change leaves the dispute resolution mechanism vague and undefined. This undermines the previously agreed upon specific procedure for dispute resolution and contradicts the intent of the original clause to first attempt resolution through a specific mediation process. It could create uncertainty about the process, court and rules to be followed in case of any disagreement between the Parties. It contradicts other sections by not mentioning any specific method.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Subject to the provision of Clause 16, the Courts having jurisdiction hereunder, shall exclusively be the courts at Mumbai, India. 15.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a detailed dispute resolution process involving mediation, which the changed text replaces with a vague reference to 'standard legal channels.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text (clause 15 and 15.1), which assumes the existence of the specific Mumbai courts having jurisdiction, undermining the previously agreed-upon procedure and the laws governing the agreement mentioned in 15.1. The change creates a clear conflict because it removes the agreed-upon method of dispute resolution, making the mentioned court jurisdiction ambiguous as to when and how it applies.
